rationale: Sell-side analysts (Jefferies, Barclays) independently frame the beauty-licence shift as a structural positive for Gucci/Kering.
Trade press flags brand-identity risk, questioning whether L'Oreal can preserve Gucci's positioning under Demna.
Factual wire report on deal timing/CEO quote with no evaluative verdict on Gucci itself.
Labour-exploitation supply-chain investigation names Gucci among probed brands; critical-angle, headline-level controversy.
Reported loss of historic Paris flagship amid rent dispute and prior global store closures; unconfirmed by Gucci/Kering so capped mild.
Kering shares down ~5.4% on the week with split analyst ratings reflecting turnaround uncertainty.
Brand-owned Instagram activation post; purely descriptive campaign content, not public opinion.
Independent forum thread shows genuinely split praise/criticism of the Monte Carlo campaign with no net verdict; no forced polarity.
Collector community recurrently compares Demna-era construction/leather quality unfavorably against Michele/Tom Ford eras.
Recirculated investigative piece on thin subcontractor margins for Gucci-style bags, corroborating this week's labor-probe coverage; discounted for age.
Trade-press catalogue of Monte Carlo campaign accessory lineup; descriptive activation write-up, no evaluative verdict.
WWD reiterates Gucci's 14.3% revenue decline with Western Europe among the weakest geographies; clear negative earnings/momentum press.
